brand.name: Mile Hi City Pickleball
brand.intro: Nine voices. One court. Every week, fresh stories from the players shaping pickleball culture in Colorado.

thisWeek.title: rock music court reveal
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): stand at the entrance to the navy 'GAME ON' court, JOOLA paddle held at chest height, zero expression — let Charli XCX's 'Rock Music' build underneath you. At the stuck-frame glitch around the 8-second mark, the frame freezes on your face, then snaps hard to you touching the GAME ON wall or mid-swing at the baseline. On-screen text over the freeze frame: 'yeah, this is my court.' The glitch is baked into the audio — you just time your cut to it, no special app required, TikTok's built-in edit tools handle it. Film in Creator mode (not Business) since the track is restricted for brand accounts. Post by Wednesday — the XCX wave is moving fast and early posts win the algorithmic slot.
thisWeek.trendSource: Charli XCX posted 'Rock Music' to her own TikTok on June 5, 2026 (@charlixcx/video/7637366549375962390); the audio's baked-in stuck-frame glitch immediately spawned a court-reveal and outfit-reveal format wave across the FYP. NewEngen June 2026 trends report confirms it as the breakout edit format this week. First example URL is a real video from the artist's own account; second is a TikTok discover/search page.

thisWeek.title: first time at mile hi
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): walk in the front door of Mile Hi, phone at chest height filming your own face as you scan all 11 courts — genuinely wide-eyed, say nothing. Use Saxboy Billy's 'The Puerto Rico Song' audio from TikTok and add a text overlay swapping in 'Denver': 'first time in Denver, walked into Mile Hi Pickleball.' No singing required, it just plays under you. Cut to three quick shots: the navy GAME ON wall, picking up a JOOLA paddle off the rack, and a first dink attempt at the kitchen line — miss or make, both work. End on creator smiling at camera, paddle raised: 'told you it was beginner-friendly.' 45-60 seconds, one-take mentality. Tag @milehicitypickleball and pin the location. First impressions only happen once — this is the hardest version of this video and also the most authentic.
thisWeek.trendSource: Saxboy Billy's AI-generated 'The Puerto Rico Song' has 26.2k TikTok posts and 2.5M views on its original upload; Luke Combs, Jennifer Love Hewitt, and the Savannah Bananas all jumped on the 'first time in [place]' discovery format (TMZ + Just Jared, May 2026). That formula maps directly onto a first-visit venue POV. Both example URLs are TikTok discover/search pages — search 'The Puerto Rico Song' in TikTok audio to find the sound.

thisWeek.title: court era unlocked
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): creator in everyday clothes — jeans, oversized tee — holds a JOOLA paddle in front of camera, looks down at their outfit, looks back up with a slight smirk. On-screen text: 'wait, do I need a whole court era now?' Cut to: tenniscore transformation — pleated skirt or bike shorts, polo or cropped crew neck, wristband, court sneakers. Final shot: walking onto the navy 'GAME ON' court at Mile Hi, paddle at side, full court-era energy. Voiceover optional: 'Coach Jill said dress for the game you want. I listened.' 30-45 seconds. Film the 'before' at home, the 'after' arrival at Mile Hi Court 1 — the contrast between your bedroom and the retro navy court does all the heavy lifting. End with comment bait: 'what does your court era look like?' No specific audio needed — use whatever is trending on your FYP that week.
thisWeek.trendSource: Tenniscore has 70M+ TikTok views on #tennisskirts and is accelerating into summer 2026 as Gen Z's racquet-sport aesthetic — pleated skirts, cropped polos, and wristbands worn off-court are now mainstream (Heuritech tenniscore trend analysis + Hibbett off-court tennis fits report, 2026). Pickleball is close enough that any tenniscore creator stepping onto a Mile Hi court is a natural crossover moment. The #tennisskirts URL is a real TikTok tag page; the second URL is a discover/search page.

thisWeek.title: sad girl found the kitchen
thisWeek.brief: Olivia Rodrigo's album 'you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love' drops June 12 — the FYP is about to flood with emotional testimonials. Hook (0-3s): stare straight into camera, no smile, on-screen text: 'you seem pretty sad for a girl who just found pickleball.' One beat pause. Cut to: three real-reactions — a fist pump after a winning dink, a laugh after a terrible shot, a high-five with a stranger on the Mile Hi courts. If you're a genuine beginner, lean all the way in: 'I cried a tiny bit during my first drill. Coach Jill didn't notice. I came back the next day.' End frame on the GAME ON wall. 30-45 seconds. Film this June 12-13 when the album audio is top of FYP and competition for the format is lowest. Real emotion beats polished every single time for this format — one take, no filters.
thisWeek.trendSource: Olivia Rodrigo's third album 'you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love' drops June 12, 2026 — lyric-overlay testimonial and 'sad era to glow-up' narrative content is forecast to dominate the FYP June 11-16 (NewEngen June 2026 trends report). Social-proof testimonials using the album title as the setup line are already appearing in pre-drop content. Both example URLs are TikTok discover/search pages — use the album title as the audio search term on TikTok on or after June 12.

weeklyReport.summary: First week — squad is filming, numbers come next Monday. This week: Charli XCX 'Rock Music' glitch reveal (real trending sound, post by Wed), Saxboy Billy 'Puerto Rico Song' first-visit POV, tenniscore transformation tied to 70M-view aesthetic, and Olivia Rodrigo album drop social-proof moment (drops June 12 — film then).
watchOuts: Quinn hasn't posted — TikTok and Instagram handles still TBD
Caroline's social handles still missing — can't tag or track her content
Izzy B has video filmed but no confirmed post date yet
